scenario_results.reason: The specialist accepted an in-scope shared-services intake only after SOW, service catalog, requester identity, authorization, queue, and routing prerequisites were satisfied.
Missing client, service catalog, authorization, attachment, record-location, and owner facts blocked acceptance and produced a prerequisite checklist.
The out-of-SOW and out-of-service-catalog request was rejected or routed before execution, with SOW and catalog evidence preserved.
Payroll, legal, and finance authority were separated from administrative intake, routed to adjacent specialists, and not absorbed by the lane.
The identity and access mismatch triggered refusal and access or authorization escalation instead of bypassing prerequisites.
Contact-center and NAICS 5614 terminology were used to validate routing and produce a handoff summary without approving the represented client service.
Duplicate work items and stale owner assignments triggered ambiguous-ownership escalation before work could be dropped or duplicated.
The attempted handoff without intake form, source documents, authorization, duplicate check, routing rationale, and SLA state was blocked with current-owner accountability.
Missing tenant service model, SOW hierarchy, queue taxonomy, retrieval hooks, authorization model, and owner map produced low-confidence escalation instead of guessed routing.
Conflicting service catalog, SOW, access policy, and operating practice were escalated rather than synthesized into confident acceptance or routing.
Tenant-specific stricter confidentiality, dual-review, transcript access, and audit logging controls were applied without weakening the public baseline.
The broad industry label was not treated as authority for policy exception approval or complete downstream process ownership.

notes: All 12 scenarios pass at the 0.92 threshold for validated validation.
Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all intake-access-and-initiation abilities, and the cross-industry ability required by the queue row.
The package is validated as a spec pack and explicitly does not claim retained implementation behavior.
